question: Why is c>1 decode far from GPU-efficiency limits, and is missing graph replay the core issue?
method: rocprofv3 --kernel-trace differential (decode-tokens 8 vs 40, warmup 8) on native BF16-KV PARO 512-prompt, c1 via qwen35_paro_bench, c4/c8 via retained-bench native_batch. Per-step decode dispatch count and profiled kernel time = (d40-d8)/32. Clean (non-profiled) c1 graph-replay vs eager decode for the authoritative graph-replay delta.

dispatch_count_per_decode_step.note: c8 issues ~10.2x the dispatches of c1, worse than the 8x row count: each decode step fires thousands of tiny kernels; dispatch count is the dominant decode wall.

c1_graph_replay_clean_delta.note: c1 paro_bench enables graph replay BY DEFAULT; this is the authoritative (non-profiler) measure of what graph replay buys at 512 context. Larger than the doc's +2.6-3.9% at 4K because short-context kernels are smaller relative to per-dispatch overhead.

reliable_findings.c_gt_1_graph_replay_status: NOT wired (execution.scheduler_metadata.graph_bucket_stats.replay_kernel_hits=0). The native c>1 batch decode step is not device-resident (per-step host token list, host positions, per-layer host->device block-table copy), so it cannot be captured/replayed as-is.

profiler_caveat: Profiled kernel-time sums are perturbed (rocprofv3 instrument/serialize short kernels): c4/c8 profiled kernel ns/step exceed the clean wall/step, so they are NOT a clean utilization measure. c1 profiled busy/step ~2.74 ms vs clean 7.5 ms wall implies <=~37% GPU utilization at c1 even WITH graph replay; treat as an upper bound. Reliable signals are dispatch counts and the clean graph-replay delta.

interpretation.core_issue_is_dispatch_count_not_only_gr: Graph replay is already on at c1 and still leaves the GPU ~63% idle; the wall is the sheer number of tiny dispatches (917/step c1, 9362/step c8), each carrying irreducible per-dispatch MEC/SPI overhead that graph replay cannot remove. Graph replay removes per-dispatch HOST latency and per-step host overhead (worth ~+20% at c1, likely more at c>1 where 9362 dispatches/step overwhelm eager host issue), but the structural fix is fewer/larger kernels.
levers_in_priority: 1. Wire c-aware (c>1) decode graph replay: make the batch decode step device-resident (device token feedback via batch_lm_out_index, on-device position/context advance, persistent device block tables/spans instead of per-step host copies), then capture/replay like the c=1 path. Bounded refactor; recovers the eager penalty (~>=20%).
2. Reduce decode dispatch count via kernel fusion (917/step at c1 is high). Fewer launches help c=1 AND c>1 beyond graph replay.

3. BIG LIFT (separate kernel-family effort): output-column-tiled c>1 GEMM. Loads each weight tile once and reuses across all c columns (caching the quantized activation once), plus per-expert token batching for MoE. This both cuts dispatch count and amortizes weight loads -- the only lever toward the c>1 roofline. Treat as its own large workstream, not part of the graph-replay change.
